result:
Yes, both Joe Orton and Bernard-Marie Koltès were playwrights.

Joe Orton was an English playwright, author, and diarist known for his scandalous black comedies, such as "Entertaining Mr Sloane," "Loot," and "What the Butler Saw." [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Joe_Orton), [2](https://www.britannica.com/biography/Joe-Orton), [3](https://www.bloomsbury.com/us/author/joe-orton/), [4](https://www.casarotto.co.uk/clients/joe-orton)His career, though short, was highly influential, and his work often featured dark, farcical cynicism.
[1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Joe_Orton)
Bernard-Marie Koltès was a celebrated French playwright and theatre director. H[5](https://howlround.com/happenings/world-theatre-bernard-marie-koltes), [6](https://martafluvia.com/en/bernard-marie-koltes/)is groundbreaking work, which includes plays like "The Night Just Before the Forests," "Sallinger," and "In the Solitude of Cotton Fields," is a staple of modern theatre and addresses contemporary issues. K[5](https://howlround.com/happenings/world-theatre-bernard-marie-koltes), [7](http://nolimitboy.blogspot.com/2011/04/bernard-marie-koltes.html), [8](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bernard-Marie_Kolt%C3%A8s)oltès is considered one of the most important voices in French theatre of his time.[8](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bernard-Marie_Kolt%C3%A8s)

direct_output:
**Process Summary:**
1.  **Initial Search (Wikipedia_RAG):** The first action attempted to retrieve biographical data using a Wikipedia RAG tool. However, this step returned no relevant pages, likely due to the specific phrasing of the query or indexing limitations for a combined search.
2.  **Secondary Search (Google):** A follow-up search using the Ground Google Search Tool was executed with the same query. This action successfully retrieved comprehensive results confirming the professions of both individuals.
3.  **Verification:** The search results explicitly identified Joe Orton as an English playwright known for black comedies and Bernard-Marie Koltès as a celebrated French playwright and theatre director.
4.  **Synthesis:** Based on the authoritative sources cited in the Google search results (including Wikipedia and Britannica), it was confirmed that both individuals share the profession of playwright.

<answer>Yes, both Joe Orton and Bernard-Marie Koltès were playwrights. Joe Orton was a renowned English playwright known for his dark comedies, while Bernard-Marie Koltès was a celebrated French playwright and theatre director.</answer>
